notes: Header: 'SECTION OF Borehole near the City of Durham Waterworks', NZ/24/SE 33; Surface Level c. 120 O.D. (pencilled '53' struck over '67' beneath the datum line, meaning unclear); Communicated by BGS 2653; Date of boring 1874; Borer Wm. Coulson & Son (reads 'Wm. Coulson Ltd.'); One-inch Map 27, Six-inch Map Durham 27 NW. Two-page log, interval numbering runs continuously (page 1 = intervals 1-27, page 2 = intervals 28-40). Coulson-style fathoms/feet/inches log: 1 fathom = 6 ft, feet cells run 0-5. The entire Depth-from-Surface column was cross-checked against previous-depth + thickness and reconciles EXACTLY at every interval (running total of thicknesses = 3559 in = 296 ft 7 in = 49 fathoms 2 ft 7 in) — NO clerk arithmetic slips found. Final depth 296 ft 7 in = 90.40 m; the survey index value 90.24 m corresponds to ~49-2-1 (it appears to drop the last 6 in of the basal bed). Drift/rockhead is at approx. interval 9/10 (~53 ft, 8 fath 5 ft): sand+gravel+clay above, Coal Measures sandstone ('post'), mudstone ('metal') and thin coals below. Vernacular: 'post' = sandstone, 'metal' = argillaceous mudstone, 'girdles' = thin interbedded sandstone bands (kept as mudstone with annotation per schema). Five thin coals logged (intervals 19, 23, 29, 31, 37); none carry an ink seam name. Pencil marginalia by a later BGS geologist annotate five horizons: '? TBT' (int.11), 'BAT' (int.15), 'H₂'/'Hn' (int.19 COAL), 'H₂ MB' (int.31 COAL), 'H₂' (int.37 COAL) — all recorded verbatim with uncertainty as their glyphs do not map cleanly to standard Durham seam abbreviations. Water noted at intervals 8 and 21. Ink blots obscure the depth feet/inch cells of intervals 37-39 but arithmetic recovers them (all =1). CORRECTION NOTE: an early over-zoomed reading of a number-only crop appeared to show a 'COAL (Hutton)' at ~33-4 with a 'Grey metal thill' below; a full-width re-crop proved this spurious — the page-1 tail is Blue metal / COAL / Grey metal with post girdles / Strong blue metal / Hard white post / Strong blue metal, ending at 31-4-1, with no ink seam name anywhere. Ignored: BGS blue banner, watermarks, and the 'BGS REGISTRATION NO. NZ 24 SE / 33' registration stamp.
